Title: Magazine-Cover Headshot From One Selfie: 85mm Editorial, Real Skin
Teaser: One plain selfie becomes a hyper-real 85mm editorial headshot with named studio lighting, true pores-and-peach-fuzz skin and creamy bokeh, ready for Nano Banana, Midjourney and Flux.
PromptHint: Upload a clear front-facing photo, then set the [VIBE/ROLE], an optional [WARDROBE] and a [BACKDROP COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a magazine-cover portrait photographer who writes surgically precise image-model prompts. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, build a hyper-real 85mm editorial HEADSHOT of this exact person, keeping their face, skin tone, hairline, eye color and bone structure unmistakably recognizable. Vibe/role: [VIBE/ROLE]. Wardrobe: [WARDROBE]. Backdrop: [BACKDROP COLOR]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where the key light sits, what separates real skin from plastic, which crop flatters this face, how to keep identity locked. SKIN + DETAIL CONSTRAINTS: keep visible pores, fine vellus hair, natural specular highlights and subtle subsurface glow; ENFORCE eyes tack-sharp with catchlights; BAN airbrushed/waxy/over-smoothed skin, extra or fused fingers, warped ears, mangled teeth, duplicated faces. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming an 85mm f/1.8 look, a 3-light studio setup (key + soft rim + low fill), catchlights at 10 o'clock, shallow depth of field, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity, photo-realistic not illustrated'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Wardrobe > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 120 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'change only X, preserve the same facial features, expression and composition' instruction. Output nothing else.

Title: Catalog-Grade Product Hero Shot From a Phone Snap, Label Razor-Sharp
Teaser: Converts a rough product snapshot into a clean studio hero shot with physically-correct reflections, a soft gradient sweep and the label kept razor-sharp and legible.
PromptHint: Upload your product photo, then name the [PRODUCT], a [SURFACE/MOOD] and an optional [BRAND COLOR].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a commercial product photographer shooting hero frames for a premium e-commerce storefront. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the exact product reference, create a hyper-real STUDIO HERO SHOT of this product: [PRODUCT]. Surface/mood: [SURFACE/MOOD]. Accent color: [BRAND COLOR]. NON-NEGOTIABLE: keep the product's real shape, material, proportions, logo and label text legible and undistorted. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): how light should wrap this material (glossy vs matte vs glass vs metal), where a soft reflection grounds it, what background makes it pop without distraction. MATERIAL + LIGHT CONSTRAINTS: physically plausible reflections and contact shadow, seamless soft-gradient backdrop, large softbox key from upper-left, gentle gradient reflection on a clean surface; BAN floating products, gibberish or altered label text, harsh blown highlights, warped geometry, duplicated product. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph describing the studio setup, a 100mm macro lens at f/8 for full sharpness, the lighting and reflection, and the literal line 'preserve the exact product, logo and label text unchanged'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Surface > Lighting > Style, ending exactly with --ar 1:1 --style raw --s 100 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'replace the background with a clean studio gradient while keeping the same product, logo, label and lighting' instruction. Output nothing else.

Title: Golden-Hour Glow Portrait: Warm Cinematic Backlight, Honest Skin
Teaser: Builds a sun-flared 85mm golden-hour portrait with a hair-rim light, filmic haze and real pores intact, plus matched Midjourney and Flux relight variants.
PromptHint: Upload the photo, then set the [LOCATION], the [TIME-OF-DAY MOOD] and an optional [OUTFIT].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a natural-light portrait photographer known for warm, filmic outdoor work. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, build a hyper-real GOLDEN-HOUR portrait of this exact person with identity fully preserved. Location: [LOCATION]. Mood: [TIME-OF-DAY MOOD]. Outfit: [OUTFIT]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): where the low sun sits to create a hair/rim light, how to retain shadow detail on the face, how much haze is allowed before it kills sharpness. LIGHT + SKIN CONSTRAINTS: backlit low-angle sun, warm ~3200K rim on hair and shoulders, soft bounced fill on the face, gentle lens flare, hazy bokeh; keep pores, peach-fuzz and natural skin sheen; eyes tack-sharp; BAN HDR-crunch, orange-teal overcook, plastic skin, double rim lights, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming an 85mm f/1.4 lens, golden-hour backlight, sharp eyes, and the literal line 'keep the same face and identity'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Location > Lighting > Lens, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 150 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'relight this portrait to warm golden-hour backlight while preserving the same face, pose and composition' instruction. Output nothing else.

Output nothing else.

Title: Disposable-Camera Flash Snapshot: 2000s Grain, Light Leaks, Timestamp
Teaser: Recreates the unmistakable point-and-shoot tell-stack: harsh on-camera flash, hot skin falloff, grain, light leaks, vignette, white border and a corner timestamp, with your face kept exact.
PromptHint: Upload the photo, then set the [SCENE/VIBE], an optional [LOCATION] and a corner [TIMESTAMP].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a photographer who deliberately shoots imperfect point-and-shoot film so images read as found memories. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, restyle this exact person as a 2000s DISPOSABLE-CAMERA flash snapshot with identity preserved. Scene/vibe: [SCENE/VIBE]. Location: [LOCATION]. Corner timestamp to render: [TIMESTAMP]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): the precise tell-stack of a real direct-flash photo - hot flash falloff, slight motion, heavy grain, color shift, vignette - and how each one defeats the too-perfect AI look. FILM CONSTRAINTS: harsh direct on-camera flash with bright skin falloff, visible grain, mild light leaks, slight overexposure near the flash, soft focus at the edges, white Polaroid-style border, a small orange corner timestamp; keep real skin and the exact same face; BAN crisp studio sharpness, clean creamy bokeh, over-clean skin, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph describing the disposable-camera flash look, grain, light leaks, white border, the literal corner timestamp '[TIMESTAMP]', and the line 'keep the exact same face'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Scene > Flash Look > Film, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 200 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'add harsh on-camera flash, film grain, light leaks and a white Polaroid border with the timestamp "[TIMESTAMP]" while keeping the same face and pose' instruction. Output nothing else.

Title: Reverse-Brief Portrait Director: It Interviews You, Then Writes the Prompt
Teaser: Flips the workflow: the model interviews you one question at a time, recommends each answer, then delivers a tailored photoreal portrait prompt across Nano Banana, Midjourney and Flux.
PromptHint: Upload your photo (optional) and state your rough goal in [WHAT I WANT], then just answer the questions it asks.
Prompt: ROLE: You are a portrait art director and prompt engineer. CONTEXT: My rough goal is [WHAT I WANT]. Do NOT write the image prompt yet. STEP 1 - INTERVIEW: ask me 5-7 diagnostic questions, ONE at a time, to nail the spec I have not thought through - purpose and platform plus aspect ratio, mood and wardrobe, lighting style (studio vs natural vs cinematic), lens and crop, background, and whether identity must be preserved from an uploaded photo. For each question, also propose the answer YOU would recommend so I can simply reply 'yes, go with that'. Wait for my reply before asking the next. STEP 2 - after I answer all (or say 'use your picks'), THINK silently about how the choices combine, then deliver. OUTPUT FORMAT (only at Step 2, print exactly these four labeled blocks): 1) SPEC RECAP: 4-6 bullet lines of the locked decisions. 2) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph with a named lens, lighting, and 'keep the same face' if a photo was uploaded. 3) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Environment > Lighting > Lens, ending with --ar, --style raw and --hd. 4) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'change only X, preserve the rest' instruction. Begin now with question 1 only, and nothing else.

Title: Collectible 1/7 Figurine of Yourself: Boxed Figure on an Acrylic Base
Teaser: The viral mini-figurine trend done premium: a 1/7-scale collectible of you on a clear acrylic base, with the 3D modeling viewport on screen behind it and your name printed crisp on a branded box.
PromptHint: Upload a clear full or half-body photo, then set [YOUR NAME], a [THEME/POSE] and an optional [BOX BRAND VIBE].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a product photographer shooting premium Japanese collectible figures. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, create a hyper-real 1/7-SCALE COLLECTIBLE FIGURINE of this exact person, keeping the face, hairstyle and outfit clearly recognizable. Pose/theme: [THEME/POSE]. Box brand vibe: [BOX BRAND VIBE]. SCENE: the figure stands on a round transparent acrylic base on a desk; behind it a PC monitor shows the 3D modeling viewport of the same figure; beside it a premium printed figure box reads '[YOUR NAME]'. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): which details read at figure scale, how studio product light hits sculpted PVC, how to keep the box text crisp and legible. MATERIAL + LIGHT CONSTRAINTS: realistic painted-PVC plastic sheen, sharp sculpt detail, soft studio product lighting, a true contact shadow on the desk, shallow depth on the box; BAN warped or gibberish box text, a second figure, a floating base, human-skin realism on the figure (it is plastic). OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph for the full scene with the literal lines 'render the box title "[YOUR NAME]" clearly' and 'keep the same face on the figure'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Pose > Scene > Lighting, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 120 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'keep the figure and box title "[YOUR NAME]" sharp and legible while preserving the same face' instruction. Output nothing else.

Title: One JSON Recipe, Same Portrait Look Across Nano Banana, Midjourney and Flux
Teaser: A reusable structured JSON recipe (subject, wardrobe, lighting, camera, mood, on-image text) that you rewrite once and render consistently on Nano Banana, Midjourney and Flux.
PromptHint: Fill the schema fields: [SUBJECT], [LIGHTING], [CAMERA], and an optional on-image [TEXT].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a prompt-systems engineer building a reusable photoreal recipe. TASK: Take my inputs - subject: [SUBJECT]; lighting: [LIGHTING]; camera: [CAMERA]; on-image text in quotes: [TEXT] - and produce a portable image recipe. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): keep the schema flat and literal about identity and structure, never flowery; every field must be a concrete phrase. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these four labeled blocks, in this order, nothing else): 1) JSON RECIPE: a single flat JSON object with keys subject, wardrobe, setting, lighting, camera (lens plus aperture), mood, color_palette, aspect_ratio, render_text - each value a concrete phrase; if no on-image text was given, set render_text to "". 2) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E: the recipe rewritten as one conversational paragraph (Nano Banana reasons over plain English; put any on-image words in DOUBLE QUOTES; add 'keep the same face' if a photo is uploaded). 3) MIDJOURNEY V7: the same recipe as one line ordered Subject > Setting > Lighting > Lens, ending with --ar (use the recipe's aspect_ratio value) --style raw --hd. 4) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'change only the swappable field, preserve the rest' instruction naming whichever recipe field is most likely to be re-rolled. HARD RULE: the subject and identity wording must be byte-for-byte identical across all four blocks. Output nothing else.

Output nothing else.

Title: Decade Time-Machine Portrait: Your Face as a 70s, 90s or Y2K Photo
Teaser: The viral retro restyle: renders your real face in an authentic 70s film, 90s or Y2K look with period grain, wardrobe and hair, identity locked and age unchanged.
PromptHint: Upload the photo, then pick the [DECADE], an optional [FILM STOCK/CAMERA] and [SETTING].
Prompt: ROLE: You are a portrait photographer who recreates authentic period photography. TASK: Using the uploaded photo as the single source of identity, restyle this exact person into a [DECADE] portrait, keeping their face, age and identity fully recognizable. Camera/film: [FILM STOCK/CAMERA]. Setting: [SETTING]. THINK FIRST (silently, do not print): what makes this era read true - grain structure, color shift, wardrobe, hairstyle and the lighting tech of the time - without altering the person. PERIOD + REALISM CONSTRAINTS: era-accurate film grain and color cast, period-correct wardrobe and hair, soft on-camera or studio lighting matching the decade, a slight imperfection so it reads as a real photo not clean AI; keep real skin texture; BAN modern over-sharpening, any change to face shape or age, anachronistic clothing, identity drift. OUTPUT FORMAT (print exactly these three labeled blocks, nothing before or after): 1) NANO-BANANA / DALL-E PROMPT: one conversational paragraph naming the decade, the film look, an 85mm or 50mm lens, period grain, and the literal line 'keep the exact same face and identity'. 2) MIDJOURNEY V7: one line ordered Subject > Era Wardrobe > Lighting > Film, ending exactly with --ar 4:5 --style raw --s 180 --hd. 3) FLUX KONTEXT EDIT: one 'apply an authentic [DECADE] film look and wardrobe while preserving the exact face, age and pose' instruction. Output nothing else.
